mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-stat for return difference versus baseline is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential with p-value greater than 0.05 over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, leading to negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of contraction periods or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months or correlation between CPI acceleration and forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative return differential or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day windows in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 or positive return frequency falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production alongside stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.
